data.title: SA Asks: Will Apple move iPhone production to U.S. rather than India?
data.description:
On Thursday, President Trump said he was disappointed with Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) decision to move iPhone manufacturing from China to India rather than the United States.

Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is in the process of migrating certain manufacturing operations to India in part to mitigate increased tariffs on Chinese-produced goods. But given Trump's reaction to the move, will Apple (APPL) decide to shift operations to the U.S. instead?

Seeking Alpha analysts Bill Maurer, Geneva Investor and Oliver Rodzianko offer their thoughts on the situation. 

Bill Maurer: I don't think Apple is going to change its plans just because of President Trump's comments. Not only would producing iPhones in the U.S. likely be extremely costly and thus impact consumers, but the current president may no longer run the country by the time Apple (AAPL) would be able to produce the smartphone domestically. Apple (AAPL) has spent years working to increase production in many places outside of China, including India, and I don't think it will abandon those plans based on a political environment that could easily change in a few years or even at the 2026 midterms.

Geneva Investor: I do not think Apple (AAPL) will radically change course on India manufacturing. Apple’s (AAPL) effort to produce iPhones in India dates to before the Trump administration. I see it as a general effort to diversify away from China, which was hit by tariffs even before the current U.S. administration and by other countries, too. With ~60% of Apple’s (AAPL) sales generated outside of the U.S., Apple (AAPL) has all the interest to diversify its supply chain.

Additionally, producing such sophisticated devices in America is not economically feasible for Apple (AAPL). Bank of America estimates that an American-produced iPhone could cost up to 90% more to make. China’s current tariffs, at 30%, are far below that amount. India’s 10% are very manageable. Unless Trump ends up de facto embargoing both China and India, Apple (AAPL) is better off not producing in America.

Not to mention that this administration has created a very uncertain future when it comes to the extent and duration of tariffs. Apple (AAPL) cannot embark on such a long-term CAPEX investment without knowing whether these tariffs will even remain in place. Even if tariffs were to be raised and maintained, there would be a significant risk of roll-back after 2026 midterm elections or 2028. I find it more likely that Apple (AAPL) will commit to some other form of investment in America to “appease” Trump, so that the administration can present it as a win.

Oliver Rodzianko: Apple (AAPL) will likely stick to its India manufacturing strategy, even though it has been pressured by President Trump to bring manufacturing back home. After all, Apple (AAPL) needs to avoid near-term margin compression. 

Foxconn's (OTCPK:FXCOF) expansion in India essentially confirms this: Apple (AAPL) will more than double iPhone manufacturing there, as Foxconn (OTCPK:FXCOF) aims for 25–30 million units in 2025. This permits resilience and cost efficiency, whereas a sharp U.S. turn would be costlier due to labor and property costs. Apple (AAPL) also has regulatory concerns with its high-margin Services segment, so it needs to do all it can to maintain its overall profitability profile right now. 

An American manufacturing boom is still a longer-term possibility, pending automation advances. Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) is constructing new American chip fabs, but Apple's (AAPL) re-shoring will wait for robotics to reach maturity. Tim Cook says iPhones won't be built in the U.S. until robotic arms have the ability to work "at scale and precision.” As automation via AI closes labor cost disparities in five to 15 years, Apple can ramp up U.S. operations, boosting growth and making our global supply chains that much more secure.

data.title: EOG Resources wins oil exploration concession for UAE shale block
data.description:
EOG Resources (NYSE:EOG) said Friday it was awarded a new onshore oil exploration concession for Unconventional Onshore Block 3 in the United Arab Emirates, where it expects to begin drilling in this year's H2.

EOG (NYSE:EOG) will hold a 100% interest in the block and will be the operator, but will coordinate with Abu Dhabi National Oil Company - ADNOC - to explore and appraise unconventional oil in the concession area, and following a three-year appraisal phase, EOG said it may enter into a production concession in which ADNOC has the option to participate.

The UCO3 concession area covers nearly 900K acres in "an over-pressured, oil prone basin" in the UAE's Al Dhafra region of Abu Dhabi.

ADNOC said the award is the first of its kind to a U.S. company and "underlines the attractiveness of Abu Dhabi’s energy sector and its position as a trusted investment destination."

The agreement was one of a number of business deals agreed between the U.S. and UAE during President Trump's visit this week.

data.title: Fifth Third Bancorp explores ways to expand crypto business - report
data.description:
Fifth Third Bancorp (NASDAQ:FITB) is weighing a deeper push into crypto-related services, including stablecoin-based cross-border transfers and integration with trading and payment networks, according to a media report dated Friday.

The Cincinnati, Ohio-based lender has already established a niche client base that uses its banking infrastructure for functions like payroll and revenue collection, Ben Hoffman, the company's chief strategy officer, told Bloomberg in an interview.

Other areas of exploration include using stablecoins to facilitate international transactions to reduce costs and boost overall efficiency, he added, noting the company is also looking to link into payment networks that support consumers' crypto purchases and digital asset trading.

The move comes after five years of Fifth Third (NASDAQ:FITB) waiting for crypto regulation to become clearer, Hoffman said, Bloomberg reported. Now, with pro-crypto U.S. President Donald Trump implementing friendly crypto rules, 'it became clear that this was the right time to engage and fortunately we’ve had a team of folks actively studying and tinkering in this space,” he said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Adobe rises as Jefferies sees estimates as 'conservative,' updates Creative Cloud
data.description:
• Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) shares rose more than 3% on Friday after investment firm Jefferies said it sees earnings estimates as “conservative.”  
  • "We are positive on ADBE's new price increase of $10 (up 11-17%) on Creative Cloud All Apps plans for both individuals and teams, effective on 6/17 or at renewal,” analyst Brent Thill wrote in a note to clients. “While already embedded in FY25 guide, these increase confidence in hitting rev guide 8.9% growth at mid-pt vs. 10.8% in FY24. These should complement Firefly plans ($10-200/[month]) and enhance ADBE's role in AI.”
  • Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) shares rose more than 3% on Friday after investment firm Jefferies said it sees earnings estimates as “conservative.”  
  • Thill has a Buy rating on Adobe and $590 price target on the stock. 
  • In addition, Adobe announced changes to its Creative Cloud subscription offering for teachers and students in the U.S.

data.title: FCC approves Verizon's purchase of Frontier Communications
data.description:
• The Federal Communications Commission approved Verizon's (NYSE:VZ) $20 billion purchase of Frontier Communication (NASDAQ:FYBR) after the telecom firm agreed to end its diversity, equity and inclusion policies. 
  • The FCC approved the transfer of licenses, according to a filing with the regulator. 
  • FCC Commissioner Brendan Carr had threatened in recent months to block deals from companies that promote DEI policies.

data.title: Apple Pay, Card, Cash all having issues
data.description:
• Apple Pay and Apple Card are both experiencing issues, as well as Apple Cash according to Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) status monitor page. 
  • All three services have yellow diamonds next to them on the page, signaling an issue.

data.title: AlphaBit Investments files for a digital asset focused debt ETF
data.description:
AlphaBit Investments has filed a proposal with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission to launch a new exchange traded fund aimed at the digital asset sector. The proposed Digital Asset Debt Strategy ETF (DADS) would invest in debt securities issued by companies and governments active in the digital asset space.

According to the SEC filing, DADS will be actively managed and come with a 1.04% total annual fund operating expense.

The fund will target four primary categories of issuers:

No. 1: Digital asset mining and data center companies – Entities that generate revenue from crypto mining or digital infrastructure.

No. 2: Direct holders of digital assets – Corporate or governmental issuers holding at least 1,000 bitcoins or offering digital asset-linked debt. 

No. 3: Platform and payment companies – Firms enabling trading or payment via digital currencies. 

No. 4: Global asset managers and financial service providers – Industry leaders promoting adoption of digital assets through tokenized products, exchanges, or financing services. 

If approved, DADS will offer investors a new way to gain exposure to the digital asset ecosystem without directly owning cryptocurrencies.

While no specific names were mentioned in the SEC filing, some names that may find themselves inside of DADS coul be shares of Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN), Mara Holdings (NASDAQ:MARA), Galaxy Digital GLXY), Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT), Hut 8 (NASDAQ:HUT), Bitfarms (NASDAQ:BITF), and Bit Digital (NASDAQ:BTBT).

data.title: U.S. stocks rise as investors digest softer inflation data, sentiment report
data.description:
Wall Street major averages rose in Friday's midday trade despite softer-than-expected inflation data, and consumer sentiment dropping to the second-lowest on record.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last up 0.3%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) was also 0.3%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was also 0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 1 basis point lower at 4.43%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.98%.  

Wall Street on Thursday ended mixed, but its benchmark S&P 500 index extended a rally to four straight days and further solidified itself in positive territory for the year.

“Markets continued to advance over the last 24 hours, as weak U.S. inflation data and lower oil prices raised expectations that the Federal Reserve would still cut rates this year,” Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

A key factor driving the rally was the U.S. PPI data for April, which showed inflation pressures were much softer than expected.

“Thursday's softer-than-expected PPI inflation print helps to confirm that inflation is moving in the right direction, especially after Tuesday's soft CPI report,” said Clark Bellin, president and chief investment officer at Bellwether Wealth. “It's encouraging that the inflation data has softened in April, especially as we brace for the potential for higher inflation over the coming months as tariffs work their way through the economy.”

U.S. import and export prices surprised to the upside in April, housing starts rose in April, and building permits dropped more than expected.

On the economic side, the University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index edged down to 50.8 in May, down from 52.2 in April. Consensus was 53.1.

data.title: Tesla technical analysis - RSI indicator in overbought territory
data.description:
Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) Relative Strength Indicator is at 71.4, indicating an overbought territory. 

RSI is a momentum indicator that measures the magnitude of recent price changes to read whether the stock is overbought or oversold. An RSI score above 70 depicts overbought, and its price may fall, whereas an RSI score below 30 means it could be oversold and might correct upwards.

Looking back, the last time the RSI indicator scaled above 70 was in December 2024 when it touched an all-time high of $479 and receded thereafter. In March 2025, the RSI indicator fell below 30, and upward price revision was seen after that.

 

 

 

 

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) stock price crossed its upper Bollinger Band on May 12, when the stock price scaled above $315, once again giving an overbought signal.

The Bollinger Bands indicator works with three bands, consisting of the middle band that tracks average price, the upper band that is average price plus two standard deviations, and the lower band that is average price minus two standard deviations.

Importantly, the stock price breaking the upper band suggests an overbought scenario, while falling below the lower band is an oversold indicator.

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares rose for six straight days but snapped the winning streak on Thursday. The rally has shrunk the stock’s YTD loss to about 15%. 

Turning to the Wall Street community, almost 16 analysts gave TSLA a Strong Buy while 7 stood with Buy whereas, 14 of them gave the stock a Hold recommendation, and 8 of them gave a Strong Sell rating.

data.title: Eli Lilly, compounders partly to blame for Novo CEO’s ouster
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) (OTCPK:NONOF) appears to have forced its CEO, Lars Fruergaard Jørgensen, to resign as the Danish pharma giant grapples with the rising dominance of its U.S. rival, Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY), and makers of compounded drugs in the booming market for weight loss therapies.

“Considering the recent market challenges, the share price decline, the Novo Nordisk Board and Lars Fruergaard Jørgensen have jointly concluded that initiating a CEO succession is in the best interest of the company and its shareholders,” the company said in a statement.

Input from the Novo Nordisk Foundation, which controls the company through its investment arm, Novo Holdings A/S, has also led to the decision.

However, multiple media outlets reported that Jørgensen was ousted. The Financial Times reported that the request to step down had surprised Jørgensen, who had, however, agreed with the board’s decision.

Under Jørgensen’s eight-year tenure as CEO, Novo’s (NVO) sales, profits, and share price have almost tripled despite the recent underperformance marked by LLY’s recent market share gains amid plateauing weekly U.S. prescriptions of the company's weight loss drug, Wegovy.

According to IQVIA data, the weekly U.S. prescriptions of Lilly’s (NYSE:LLY) weight loss therapy Zepbound have approached 339K for the week ending April 18, surpassing those of Wegovy by more than 127K. The Indiana-based drugmaker has also captured 63% of the U.S. weight loss drug market, Reuters reported, citing IQVIA data.

“It’s easy with hindsight to look back and see things that didn’t turn out exactly as intended,” Jørgensen told FT, adding that he had no regrets about the decisions made as the CEO.

Novo (NVO), once Europe’s largest company in terms of market cap, thanks to its highly popular weight loss drug semaglutide, marketed as Ozempic and Wegovy, has lost more than half of its value since mid-2024.

Meanwhile, Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) has lost ~20% as both companies struggled to keep up with the surging demand for their weight loss drugs, partly met by compounders such as Hims & Hers Health (NYSE:HIMS) during supply constraints.

While the shortages are officially over in the U.S., Novo Nordisk (NVO) lowered its 2025 growth outlook for sales and operating profit this month, citing a longer-than-expected impact from compounded versions.

With its leadership now in transition amid LLY's market share gains, investors will be keenly watching how the company can return to its former glory.
